---
title: "Graph Generator"
output: pdf_document
date: "2026-01-29"
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(jsonlite)

data <- read.csv("name.csv")
years <- ## Set years
json_data <- fromJSON("name.json")

data.year <- setNames(
  lapply(years, function(y) {
   read.csv(paste0("data_", y, ".csv"))
 }), years)

```

```{r}

## Data frame
variable_name <- data$column
variable_map <- list(
variable_name = data.frame())

```

```{r}

graph_generator <- function(data, top_n = 10, x_label = NULL, title_label = NULL) {
  
  col_name <- colnames(data)[1]
  
  count_df <- data %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[col_name]])) %>%
    count(.data[[col_name]]) %>%
    slice_max(n, n = top_n)
  
  ggplot(count_df, aes(x = fct_reorder(.data[[col_name]], n, .desc = TRUE), y = n)) +
    geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(
      x = ifelse(is.null(x_label), col_name, x_label),
      y = "Number of Metric",
      title = ifelse(is.null(title_label), paste("Number of Metric by", col_name), title_label)
    )
}

```
```{r}

function_map <- list(
  graph_generator = graph_generator
)

func_name <- json_data[["function"]]
arg_name  <- json_data$arguments$x

func <- function_map[[func_name]]
arg  <- variable_map[[arg_name]]

result_plot <- do.call(func, list(data = arg))
print(result_plot)

```
